events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a commercial HVAC-R project where brazing & evacuation work is at the rough-complete stage. The observed field condition is that the vacuum did not hold at target microns during a decay test (the gauge was read, and it failed). This means the system has a leak or moisture issue that prevents proper evacuation.

Key facts:
- Vacuum not held at target microns (decay test failed)
- Work is rough-complete
- References: mfr specs, ACCA, EPA 608

Decision: Fail — the vacuum decay test not holding at target microns means the system is non-compliant with manufacturer specs and industry standards (ACCA). The system cannot be charged and put into service until it holds the required vacuum level.

Risk: Medium to high — a system that doesn't hold vacuum could have leaks (refrigerant loss, environmental/EPA 608 concerns) or moisture (compressor damage, system failure). I'd say high.

Component conditions: "non-compliant" and possibly "installed-defective" since the system was installed but the evacuation didn't pass.

Percent complete: Rough-complete stage, maybe 70-80% for the brazing & evacuation scope. The evacuation failed, so the work isn't complete. I'd estimate around 75%.

Value: The task mentions "read the gauge, FD" — FD likely means "field data" or the reading. Since no specific micron value is given, I'll use 0 or leave it. Actually, the scenario says "vacuum not held at target microns" but doesn't give a specific number. I'll set value to 0 since no specific numeric reading was provided.

Confidence: High — 0.9, since the decay test failure is clear.

Findings:
- Vacuum decay test failed — system does not hold at target microns, indicating leaks or moisture
- System non-compliant with manufacturer evacuation requirements

Actions:
- Re-pressurize and leak test (nitrogen pressure test) to locate leaks
- Repair any found leaks
- Re-evacuate to required micron level and re-perform decay test
- Verify system holds vacuum per manufacturer specs before charging

Rationale: The vacuum decay test is a critical acceptance criterion. Failure means the system has leaks or residual moisture, making it non-compliant with manufacturer specs and ACCA standards. EPA 608 also requires leak repair before charging.

References: mfr specs (evacuation requirements), ACCA (installation standards), EPA 608 (leak repair requirements).

    "Verify digital vacuum gauge operation and isolation valve integrity before retest",
    "Do not charge refrigerant until decay test passes"
  ],
  "rationale": "A failed vacuum decay test at rough-complete stage means the system does not meet manufacturer evacuation requirements or ACCA/EPA 608 best practices. Non-condensables or moisture remaining in the system risk compressor damage and reduced performance. The work is non-compliant until leakage is eliminated and target microns are held.",

    "ACCA installation standards for refrigerant piping evacuation",
    "EPA 608 refrigerant handling requirements"
  ]
}
